_comment: Approximate years when each word's semantic shift became dominant usage. Sources: OED, etymonline, historical corpus studies. Only words with reasonably datable breakpoints are included. Within-decade accuracy is used for evaluation (|pred - true| <= 10).

mouse.source: OED: first attested 1965 (Engelbart), dominant ~1984 with Mac launch

corn.source: OED: American usage 'maize' documented from early 17c colonial records

bead.source: OED: 'prayer' sense obsolete by ~1400; small sphere dominant

bad.source: Green's Dictionary of Slang: 'bad' = excellent attested from 1950s, mainstream ~1970s

horn.source: OED: musical instrument sense documented from ~1600 via Metonymy from animal horn

voyage.source: OED: Narrowed to sea-voyage ~1600 from general 'journey'

holiday.source: OED: religious feast day → secular leisure day ~1500

humour.source: OED: bodily fluid sense obsolete; 'funny' sense dominant by ~1700

see.source: OED: 'perceive mentally' documented from Old English

understand.source: OED: 'comprehend mentally' from Old English via Metonymy
